_readme: Copy this to config.json and fill in your values. Keys starting with _ are comments and are ignored by the tracker. Route #1 below is fully annotated field-by-field — routes #2 and #3 reuse the exact same field order/shape with shorter notes, so you can compare them side by side or copy-paste a block as a starting point.
_dashboard_url_note: Optional. Your live GitHub Pages dashboard URL — when set, email and WhatsApp notifications include a link straight to the route's card (e.g. '<dashboard_url>#route-<route id>') so you can tap through for the full collapsible tree and price chart that don't fit in a text message.

notifications._section: Global notification channels — enable/configure the ones you want. Each route can also override 'channels' individually below.
notifications._error_reports_note: 'receive_error_report' (on email/ntfy/whatsapp-recipient) opts that channel/person into the end-of-run error summary — a separate, route-independent notice sent only when one or more routes threw an error this run. Defaults to false everywhere so adding someone for daily/weekly digests doesn't also sign them up for error spam. Kept as the same property name across all three channels because whatsapp is already multi-recipient and email/ntfy may become so too.

whatsapp._info: Free via CallMeBot. Each recipient must individually send 'I allow callmebot to send me messages' to +34 644 54 29 97 on WhatsApp to get their own API key — there's no broadcast endpoint, one request per person.
whatsapp._per_route_note: Pick which of these recipients get notified per route via routes[].notifications.channels.whatsapp — true (all), false (none), or a list of names e.g. ["cristian", "alice"].

ntfy._info: Free push notifications. Install ntfy app, subscribe to your topic.

routes._info: ═══ ROUTE #1 — fully annotated reference. Every field below has a matching _..._note explaining it. Routes #2/#3 use this exact same field order without repeating the notes. ═══
routes.label: Bucharest → Auckland (Feb–Mar 2027)
routes._enabled_note: Set to false to fully pause this route — no searches, no notifications, no dashboard card — without deleting/commenting it out. Defaults to true if omitted. Handy for 'not interested this week, keep the config around' situations.
routes._origin_destination_note: Single IATA code (e.g. 'OTP') or a list of codes to cover a multi-airport city in one search, e.g. 'destination': ['NRT', 'HND'] for Tokyo. fli/Google Flights searches all listed airports together; the dashboard shows the actual airport each option uses. To do an open-jaw / multi-city trip (fly into one city, out of another), replace origin/destination with 'trip_type': 'multi_city' + a 'legs' array — see route #3 (otp-hkg-nrt-2027) below.
routes._max_return_date_note: Optional hard cap on return date e.g. '2027-04-15'. Useful when date_to + target_nights would spill into the next month. null = no cap.
routes._flexibility_note: Tries target-2, target, target+2 night durations per departure date
routes._departure_days_note: Weekdays to depart on e.g. ['wednesday','thursday','friday']. Empty = any day. When set, every matching weekday in the window is checked (daily_samples is ignored).
routes._return_days_note: Weekdays to return on e.g. ['sunday','monday']. Empty = any day within the flexibility window.
routes._samples_note: Number of departure dates sampled per daily run, spread evenly across the window. Ignored when departure_days is set (every matching weekday is checked instead).
routes._top_n_note: How many cheapest outbound options fli expands into return-trip searches. Higher = more complete results (e.g. catches airlines like TK that aren't in the first 5) but more requests per search and a longer run. Default 20.
routes._search_retries_note: Google's backend occasionally returns an empty payload for a date pair that has flights (confirmed: identical retried queries return the exact same stable results). Number of extra attempts (2s delay between each) before accepting 'no results' as final. Default 3.
routes._search_country_note: ISO country code for Google Flights (gl= param). 'RO' = Romania — makes Google show airlines popular from Bucharest like Turkish. Leave empty for generic results.
routes._airlines_note: List of preferred airline IATA codes e.g. ['TK', 'QR']. Empty = no preference.
routes._alliances_note: List of preferred alliances: 'ONEWORLD', 'SKYTEAM', 'STAR_ALLIANCE'. Empty = no preference. Only takes effect in 'hard' mode (passed straight to fli's alliance filter) — 'soft' mode can't flag alliance matches since fli has no airline→alliance membership data.
routes._airline_mode_note: off = show all, no flagging | soft = show all, flag matching airlines with 🏷️ (alliances not flaggable) | hard = only show flights matching a preferred airline and/or alliance (passed to fli directly)
routes._stopovers_note: Maximum number of stops. Remove or set null to allow any.
routes._layover_note: Max single layover duration in hours. Remove or set null to allow any.
routes._outbound_duration_note: Max outbound flight duration in hours (filtered client-side). Remove or set null to allow any.
routes._return_duration_note: Max return flight duration in hours (filtered client-side). Remove or set null to allow any.
routes._self_transfer_note: true = only show protected single-ticket itineraries. false = also show self-transfer (you buy 2 separate tickets, no protection if you miss connection).
routes._allow_agency_fares_note: Itineraries that aren't bookable directly through an airline (only sold by agencies/OTAs that assemble multi-carrier interline combos — no one to call but the agency if something goes wrong) are dropped by default. Set 'allow_agency_fares': true on a route to see them too. Single-carrier round trips are assumed airline-direct for free; multi-carrier ones are verified via an extra fli get_booking_options() call each (capped per route per run).
routes._bags_note: Checked bags to include in price filter (0, 1, or 2). Long-haul usually includes bags.

departure_window._mode_note: hard = exclude flights outside window | soft = show all but flag preferred ones with ⭐

routes._max_per_airline_note: Max options shown per airline group in the dashboard. Default 3.
routes._price_note: Sends an immediate alert when price drops below this. Set to null to disable.

daily._info: Sends every day if no max_price_alert set. If threshold is set, only when price is below it.

price_alert._info: Fires immediately when price drops below max_price_alert. Max once per day.

weekly_summary._days: monday | tuesday | wednesday | thursday | friday | saturday | sunday
weekly_summary._heartbeat_note: Doubles as a job heartbeat — fires on the chosen day even if zero flights matched this week (a short 'no matches, job ran fine' notice instead of the full price report), and always includes a 7-day raw-API-health line (results returned before client-side filtering) so a sudden drop to zero signals the API going quiet rather than your filters being too strict.

channels._note: Optional — overrides the route's default 'channels' below for the weekly digest only (e.g. send daily updates to everyone but the weekly summary to just yourself). Omit this whole 'channels' key to just reuse the route default. Same shape/rules as the route-level 'channels'.

channels._info: Per-route channel overrides. false = disable this channel for this route only.
channels._ntfy_topic_note: Optional: per-route ntfy topic. Leave empty to use global topic.

routes._info: ═══ ROUTE #2 — short-haul example, same field order/shape as route #1 above (see it for full notes on every field). ═══
routes.label: Bucharest → London (Summer 2027)

routes._info: ═══ ROUTE #3 — open-jaw / multi-city example: fly into one city, out of another, priced as ONE itinerary by Google (much cheaper than booking the two one-ways separately). Same field order/shape as routes #1-2 above, except 'origin'/'destination' are replaced by 'trip_type' + 'legs' — see _trip_type_note and _legs_note below. ═══
routes.label: Bucharest → Hong Kong / Tokyo → Bucharest (open-jaw, 2027)
routes._trip_type_note: 'multi_city' switches this route to fli's TripType.MULTI_CITY and requires a 'legs' array instead of top-level origin/destination. Currently supports exactly 2 legs (open-jaw: out + back via different cities).
routes._legs_note: Exactly 2 legs: [outbound, inbound]. Each is {origin, destination} — single IATA code or list of codes (multi-airport city) just like a round-trip route's origin/destination. They don't have to match: leg 1 flies OTP→HKG, leg 2 flies NRT→OTP — Google prices the whole open-jaw together.
routes._date_note: date_from/date_to/departure_days/daily_samples sample leg 1's departure date, exactly like a round-trip route's outbound. target_nights/flexibility_days/return_days/max_return_date then generate leg 2's departure date relative to leg 1 — same logic as a round-trip's return date, just read as 'days until the journey home' rather than 'nights at the destination'.

channels._whatsapp_note: List of recipient names from notifications.whatsapp.recipients to notify for this route — true = everyone, false = no one.
